text: ScoutPi is a Pi-native generic spatial runtime. Investigation examples remain typed specifications and workflows rather than product-specific branches.
Keep only earth_workspace, python_analysis and earth_story on the model-facing surface. Detailed adapter, export and evidence contracts are requested on demand.
High-risk execution requires a direct user decision and a short-lived receipt bound to the exact tool call and normalized parameters. Model-authored confirmation booleans never grant permission.

A successful investigation can be compiled into a deterministic workflow with adapter fingerprints, cost assertions, expected artifacts and explicit recovery actions. Replay checks drift before execution.
Browser text is untrusted evidence, not policy. It must be normalized, artifactized and bound to a claim or hypothesis before EarthStory can cite it.
A dry run validates contracts, plans and artifact paths but does not produce a scientific measurement. The Evidence Reviewer blocks any story that describes dry-run output as computed evidence.
Context candidates are ranked by task relevance, trust and confidence. The current turn receives a mixed-text token-bounded Context Pack while full memory remains outside the model request.
After an interrupted local export, restart recovery marks the detached job failed and retryable. Remote provider tasks remain running and must be queried before any retry.

MapLibre and Cesium consume the same typed region, observable role, year, legend and short-lived Earth Engine tile contract. Switching renderers does not require another computation or GeoTIFF download.
New executable algorithms enter through a reviewed Backend Plugin SDK with typed manifests, cancellation, timeouts and result limits. The model cannot load arbitrary runtime modules.
The operator prefers compact table spacing and restrained colors in internal dashboards.
A separate learning exercise used a PostGIS table named shanghai_pois and a GiST spatial index.
